description:
## What
Create the `lists` and `list_accounts` tables via migration and implement all five CRUD endpoints for list management.

## Why
Before list timelines or membership can work, the data model and management API must exist.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/lists and /api/v1/lists/:id to capture the exact JSON shape of the List object (id, title, replies_policy); check which HTTP methods and request body shapes are used for POST and PUT; verify the replies_policy enum values ('followed', 'list', 'none').
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write a SQLite migration adding `lists (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, persona_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES personas(id), title TEXT NOT NULL, replies_policy TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'list' CHECK(replies_policy IN ('followed','list','none')), created_at INTEGER NOT NULL)` and `list_accounts (list_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES lists(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, account_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, PRIMARY KEY(list_id, account_id))`; implement GET /api/v1/lists (all lists for authed persona), POST /api/v1/lists (create, enforce 50-list limit, return 422 if at limit), GET /api/v1/lists/:id (404 if not owned by authed persona), PUT /api/v1/lists/:id (update title and/or replies_policy), DELETE /api/v1/lists/:id (cascade deletes list_accounts rows).
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests using live mastodon.social /api/v1/lists responses as fixture JSON to validate serialization shape; test CRUD round-trip; test 50-list limit returns 422; test that fetching another persona's list returns 404.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social live fixture — curl /api/v1/lists with a real token, capture JSON, use as shape reference for serialization tests.
- Test approach: create list via POST, assert response matches fixture shape; GET list by id, assert fields match; PUT to rename, assert updated; DELETE, assert GET returns 404; attempt 51st list, assert 422.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Enforce persona ownership on all read/write/delete operations — never allow cross-persona list access
- 50-list limit enforced atomically with a SELECT COUNT before INSERT (or a CHECK constraint + unique index)
- replies_policy value must be validated against the enum; reject unknown values with 422
- id is a snowflake string in JSON, not a raw integer
title: DB schema and CRUD endpoints for lists
